schur.title: Beutelspacher & Brestovanský (1982): Generalised Schur Numbers
schur.summary: Establishes lower bounds for the generalised Schur numbers S(r) via explicit r-partition constructions of {1,...,N} with no monochromatic solution to x+y=z. Foundational reference for SA-based partition search initialisation.

schur.title: Exoo (1994): A Lower Bound for Schur Numbers and Multicolor Ramsey Numbers
schur.summary: Constructs explicit witnesses for S(r) ≥ N using a recursive algebraic partitioning scheme. Introduces the 'SA + repair' paradigm: use stochastic search seeded from an algebraic partitioning rule, then correct violations by local recolouring.

schur.title: Fredricksen (1979): Schur's Theorem in Sum-Free Sets
schur.summary: Proves tight bounds on sum-free partition density. Key insight: monochromatic Schur triples (x,y,x+y) are detected by scanning each colour class for violations of the form f(i)=f(j)=f(i+j).

partition.title: Abbott & Hanson (1972): A Problem of Schur and Its Generalizations
partition.summary: Generalises Schur's theorem to broader partition regularity settings. Proves that for any finite colouring of positive integers, a monochromatic solution exists. Relevant for understanding why exhaustive symbolic search is required at large N.

ramsey.title: Radziszowski (2021): Small Ramsey Numbers (Electronic Journal of Combinatorics Survey)
ramsey.summary: Authoritative survey of known exact values and bounds for R(r,s). Table 1 contains all confirmed values through R(4,9). Critical reference for choosing the right vertex count for any new lower-bound search.

ramsey.title: Exoo (1989): R(4,6) ≥ 36 via Circulant Graph on Z_35
ramsey.summary: Exhibits the Exoo witness: a 2-coloring of K_35 with no red K_4 and no blue K_6. The witness is a circulant graph with connection set S ⊂ Z_35. This is the canonical example for circulant + SA search on Ramsey problems.

ramsey.title: Paley Graph Construction for Ramsey Lower Bounds
ramsey.summary: If p ≡ 1 (mod 4) is prime, the Paley graph Paley(p) is self-complementary and often serves as a Ramsey witness. Connect vertices i,j iff (i-j) is a quadratic residue mod p. R(3,5)≥14 uses Paley(13); R(4,4)≥18 uses Paley(17).

graph.title: Computational Construction of Ramsey Graphs via SA (Bollobás & others)
graph.summary: Details the energy-based Metropolis criterion for graph coloring: E = |{(u,v,w): monochromatic triangle in some color}|, minimised by swapping single edge colors. Convergence to E=0 yields a valid Ramsey witness.

lean.title: Lean 4 decide Tactic for Finite Decidable Propositions
lean.summary: The `decide` tactic reduces any Decidable proposition to a concrete Bool computation evaluated by the Lean kernel. Ideal for Ramsey/Schur witnesses: `exact ⟨f, by decide⟩` proves ∃ f, ∀ x y z, ... in seconds for N≤100, minutes for N≤500.

lean.title: Finset.card_le_card (Mathlib)
lean.summary: If h : s ⊆ t then Finset.card_le_card h : s.card ≤ t.card. Useful for bounding color class sizes in partition arguments.

formal.title: Machine-Checked Combinatorics: Lean 4 Kernel Verification
formal.summary: Proofs of finite graph and partition properties can be kernel-verified by reducing to `decide` over Fin types. Key rule: never use `native_decide` (adds C compiler to TCB). Use `decide` with increased `maxHeartbeats` instead.
